about.heading: Setting technical direction, then making it ship.
paragraphs: I'm a software architect and engineering leader who breaks big monolithic applications into smaller, independent services, shapes how systems talk to each other, and builds in security and scale from the start.

I'm known for setting technical direction, mentoring teams through design and code reviews, and turning complex business needs into simple, maintainable solutions — most recently leading architecture for large airline platforms at Emirates.

roles.title: Senior Technical Lead — Software Architect track
bullets: Lead architecture and technical direction for large airline platforms (Emirates), guiding system design and a team of 7+ developers and QA engineers.
Led the move of a large monolithic booking platform (ResConnect) into smaller, independent services — splitting frontend and backend so each can be built and released on its own.
Designed domain-driven, stateful microservices for the Booking and Ancillary domains using Java 17 and Spring Boot.
Designed the API gateway and security setup, with one central place to check access tokens so services can talk to each other safely.

Used event-based messaging (Kafka & Solace) to pause and resume screen recording while payment details are entered — so sensitive information is never captured. Used Couchbase for fast, distributed data access.
Strengthened platform security by fixing vulnerability and penetration-test findings, and mentored engineers through design and code reviews.

roles.title: Senior Software Engineer
bullets: Designed and delivered large content-publishing platforms, owning database design, backend services and search.
Built scalable REST APIs with Spring, Struts 2 and Hibernate; designed Apache Solr search with indexing and query optimization for high-volume content.
Engineered automated content-aggregation crawlers (Selenium) across 200+ sources and data pipelines for ingestion, transformation and delivery.
Developed CMS web interfaces with Vue.js and AngularJS; optimized MySQL queries and indexing for high-volume processing.
Led a small team, ensuring code quality, system stability and timely delivery.

roles.title: Executive — R&D
bullets: Designed XML-based automation workflows for publishing systems, automating Adobe InDesign typesetting via the JavaScript SDK.
Built XML transformation pipelines using Java, XSLT and XPath, plus QA automation for validation and content-quality checks.

projects.heading: Systems built to scale, secure & release.
projects.intro: 14 projects across three companies plus a personal AI POC — from a multi-year airline microservices program to content automation at news scale, XML-driven publishing automation, and an agentic quote-processing platform. Each with a high-level architecture sketch.

groups.label: Personal · In progress
groups.title: QuoteAssist — AI Quote Processing
groups.summary: A self-directed POC exploring agentic AI, Elixir/OTP and event-driven design.

items.name: QuoteAssist
items.summary: An AI platform that ingests travel quote requests from Outlook or web forms, extracts structured data, enforces corporate policies, generates pricing-ready JSON and drafts intelligent email replies — designed to self-improve and graduate trusted patterns to auto-send.

features.title: Multi-channel ingestion
features.desc: Outlook Add-in, web forms and webhooks, normalised through one pipeline.

features.title: AI extraction + confidence scoring
features.desc: LLM parses free-form text into structured fields; low-confidence ones flag for human review.

features.title: Policy enforcement & smart drafts
features.desc: RAG-based corporate rules gate the quote; composes a response — or a targeted clarification email for missing fields.

features.title: Adaptive learning → auto-send
features.desc: Agent corrections become training signals; patterns graduate to auto-send, with a kill switch on confidence drop.

direct.text: Feedback loop — agent corrections become labelled training signals that fine-tune the model and raise per-pattern confidence, graduating trusted patterns to auto-send.

groups.label: Emirates · via Coforge
groups.title: Airline Agent Booking Platform — ResConnect
groups.summary: A multi-year program modernizing Emirates' agent booking system from a monolith into secured, independently deployable microservices.

items.name: Microservices Transformation
items.summary: Contributing to the incremental decomposition of a legacy monolith booking system into microservices — scoped across four active modules with hands-on architecture and implementation.

features.title: Ancillary Services — Bags
features.desc: Designed and implemented UI and backend modules for Bags within the Ancillary Services domain, covering end-to-end feature delivery across the stack.

features.title: TIBCO → Wasola migration
features.desc: Migrated legacy SOAP-based TIBCO integrations to RESTful Wasola services by extracting transformation logic and orchestration into a dedicated microservice — enabling clean, incremental removal of the monolith without a big-bang rewrite.

features.title: Data layer optimisation
features.desc: Targeted optimisation of the data layer to improve query performance and throughput across transactional and distributed data stores.

direct.text: Direct link — the Monolith (legacy core), Booking Service and Ancillary Service each connect straight to Oracle · Couchbase · Solace.

items.name: Authentication — Access Token & Sidecar
items.summary: Centralized authentication for the microservices platform — a token-validation service applied consistently to every service via the sidecar pattern.

features.title: Access-token validation service
features.desc: A dedicated microservice securing API access across the platform.

features.title: Sidecar pattern
features.desc: Consistent authentication attached to each service without duplicating auth logic.

features.title: Secure service-to-service
features.desc: Trusted communication between services in a distributed architecture.

features.title: Stronger enforcement
features.desc: Integrated with Azure AD (Spring Cloud Azure AD) and JWT for centralized, uniform security.

diagram.head: High-level architecture — sidecar auth · pod view

items.name: Legacy Development & Enhancement
items.summary: Feature development & production support on the high-traffic monolith — flight booking, agent workflows and ancillary services.

items.name: Monolith Modularization
items.summary: Refactored the monolith into independent front-end and back-end repositories — a modular foundation for the microservices migration, retaining a single deployable.

items.name: Access Management — RBAC
items.summary: Role-based access control across applications: role-location-permission mapping in Couchbase with a React.js admin UI for roles & permissions.

items.name: UI Modernization — Microfrontend POC
items.summary: Proof of concept modernizing the agent UI with a microfrontend architecture — modular UI for independent development, integrating AngularJS with React.

groups.label: Hindustan Times
groups.title: Content Platforms — Crawl, Search & Deliver
groups.summary: A decade building content automation, search and delivery systems operating at news scale.

items.name: UI-Based Auto Crawling & Scraping Platform
items.summary: An end-to-end content automation platform — crawl, scrape, template and deliver articles across the full writer, QA and client review lifecycle.

features.title: Four integrated systems
features.desc: Configuration management, crawling & scraping, article templating and delivery — working as one workflow.

features.title: Rule-driven configuration
features.desc: Define per-site scraping rules with CSS selectors and XPath (Spring Boot, Vue.js, Quasar).

features.title: Crawling & scraping engine
features.desc: Selenium and HTMLUnit extracting structured data from websites.

features.title: Templating & delivery
features.desc: Transform raw data into structured content, then distribute via FTP and RSS.

items.name: HTS Portal — Large-Scale Content & Search
items.summary: A content platform managing millions of articles and images across multiple publications, with high-volume daily ingestion and fast search.

features.title: Application architecture
features.desc: Spring DI, Struts 2 (MVC) and Hibernate ORM, with a Bootstrap web interface.

features.title: Ingestion pipeline
features.desc: Fetch articles and images from FTP and store them with metadata.

features.title: Solr search
features.desc: Indexing, filtering and high-performance query execution at scale.

features.title: MySQL tuning
features.desc: Optimized queries and indexing to improve response time and scalability.

items.name: NewZZa Mobile Backend
items.summary: REST backend serving articles & media to mobile apps, with Solr-indexed retrieval and filtering.

items.name: NMS — Article Delivery via RSS
items.summary: Configuration-driven RSS delivery for external clients, with secure Basic-Auth endpoints and dynamic feeds.

items.name: NMS — RSS Splitter
items.summary: Spring Boot console app parsing and splitting RSS feeds into category feeds on a schedule, with per-feed limits.

groups.label: Thomson Digital & earlier
groups.title: Publishing Automation & Systems
groups.summary: Where it started — automating typesetting from XML, and building real-world systems end to end.

items.name: Adobe InDesign Auto Pagination
items.summary: An XML-driven pagination workflow built from scratch to automate InDesign typesetting for publishers like Elsevier and Pearson, each with their own XML DTDs.

features.title: Auto pagination (80–90%)
features.desc: Automation scripts auto-fill and typeset pages up to 80–90% using the InDesign JavaScript SDK.

features.title: XML→XML transformation
features.desc: Transformation engine built with Java, XSLT and XPath against publisher DTDs.

features.title: Full layout automation
features.desc: Auto table & image placement, bookmarking, linking, XML-out and index-page generation.

features.title: Workflow integration
features.desc: Integrated the automation scripts into the InDesign production workflow.

items.name: Centralised Kiosk Display & Ad Management
items.summary: Scheduled advertisement display on Linux-based kiosks with an ad-wallet — debit on a successful play, refund on failure. Deployed at Rajasthan bus terminals and malls.

items.title: Zero-impact TIBCO → Wasola migration
items.desc: Migrated stateless services using a microservices + feature-flag strategy with no system downtime — recognized by the client.

items.title: Monolith → Microservices transformation
items.desc: Led a step-by-step shift from one large application to smaller services, shaping the service design the whole platform was built on.

items.title: AI-assisted delivery
items.desc: Adopted AI tooling for unit-test generation and legacy-code comprehension, measurably improving delivery efficiency.

education.heading: Foundations.

items.school: Board of Technical Education, Uttar Pradesh

education.now: Architecting and implementing image offload for Dnata Travel (via Coforge) — migrating binary assets from SQL Server BLOB columns to Azure Blob Storage, building .NET APIs for zero-downtime cutover and dual-read compatibility.
